data.title: Filament Health to raise C$0.9M, delist from Cboe Canada
data.description:
Clinical‐stage natural psychedelic drug development company, Filament Health (NEOE:FH:CA) (OTCQB:FLHLF) expects to complete the financing for net proceeds of approximately C$0.9M.

The company also announced that it intends to delist its common shares voluntarily from Cboe Canada, after obtaining necessary shareholder approval.

Pursuant to the financing, the company will issue a unit comprised of a secured convertible debenture of the company and a warrant for the purchase of common shares of the company.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures slide as tariff driven selloff continues; eyes on labor data
data.description:
The gloomy mood on Wall Street is expected to persist, with stock index futures dropping on Friday as President Donald Trump's latest tariffs continue to spook markets. Investors are also awaiting key labor data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.9%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 9 basis points to 3.96%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 6 basis points to 3.66%.

U.S. stocks were mauled Thursday, with the S&P 500 closing in a correction as President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs stoked fears of a global trade war. The S&P 500 closed at -4.8%, finishing its worst session since June 2020.

The selloff occurred after Trump late Wednesday detailed new tariffs that the U.S. is enacting on imports. The minimum baseline tariff will be 10%.

"The last 24 hours have been truly historic for markets, as the impact of the U.S. reciprocal tariffs cascaded across different asset classes, with no sign of letting up overnight," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"In terms of what happens now, the big question is how the U.S.’s trading partners might retaliate, as that will play a huge role in determining what the overall economic and market impact will be… On the back of all this, investors grew increasingly fearful about a potential US recession, with U.S. equities seeing their sharpest decline in years," Allen added.

Financial markets expected a significant tax increase from Trump. Yesterday’s reaction shows the tax increase was worse than anticipated. U.S. dollar weakness is telling. We often hear that when the U.S. sneezes the global economy catches cold. This is not the US sneezing. This is the U.S. cutting off its own arm, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"For now, US recession risks have risen. Over the longer term, effective tariff rates should fall somewhat. Rerouting supply chains, shifting demand patterns, and the political and economic pressure on Trump to retreat should reverse some of this damage," Donovan added. 

Traders will keep their eyes on the important March jobs report slated to come before the bell. Economists expect job additions to fall to 135K from 151K in February and the unemployment rate to stay level at 4.1%.

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is expected to speak at the SABEW conference in Arlington, Virginia.

data.title: Nvidia takes part in $300M funding round of AI video startup Runway
data.description:
AI video startup Runway AI secured over $300M in funding from investors including Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA).

The New York-based startup said the funding was led by General Atlantic with participation from Fidelity Management & Research Company, Baillie Gifford, Nvidia, SoftBank Vision Fund 2 and other new and existing investors.

The funding values the company at $3 Billion, Bloomberg News reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Runway also announced an expansion of Runway Studios —  its AI film and animation studio dedicated to producing original content using the foundation models made by its research team.

The company noted that the money will help it expand and advance its research in media generation and support in developing new AI research for new media. Runway added that it continues to hire researchers and engineers.

Recently, Runway had unveiled its latest media generation model called Gen-4 which can precisely generate consistent characters, locations and objects across scenes, according to the company.

Runway’s software has been used for projects, including generating some scenes for Amazon's (AMZN) House of David, creating visuals for a Madonna concert tour and producing an ad for Puma, according to the Bloomberg report.

The company has also signed an agreement with Lionsgate to train an AI model on the studio’s content which can be used in its film projects. Runway's Co-Founder and CEO Cris Valenzuela said the company is partnering with more studios to make films with them, but he did not disclose their names, the report added.

data.title: China's Niu Technologies reports 57% increase in Q1 sales
data.description:
• NIU (NASDAQ:NIU) sold 203,313 units in the first quarter of 2025, up 57% from a year ago.
  • The company sold 183,065 units in China and 20,248 units in the international markets.
  • In China, the company achieved sales volume growth of over 60% year-over-year.
  • Furthermore, the launch of the NX Pro in late March received an overwhelmingly positive market reception, generating massive pre-order demand across all sales channels and attracting over 3.5 million viewers during its live debut broadcast, the company said.

data.title: Amazon launches 'Buy for me' AI agent that shops on other sites for you
Yardeni sees 'great buying opportunities' after tariff-fueled stock market crash
data.description:
President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs stunned the stock market, with over $2T wiped out from the S&P 500 (SP500) on Thursday in its worst single-day loss since 2020. But Wall Street veteran Edward Yardeni sees this as an opportunity.

"I think some great buying opportunities are being created here," the founder of Yardeni Research said in a Bloomberg TV interview, as the market "is giving a big thumbs down to this tariff policy."

Yardeni said Trump's willingness to "listen to phenomenal offers" from U.S. trading partners could lead to some concessions from their end. "Hopefully, this whole thing will be behind us within the next 3-6 months. Maybe that's too optimistic."

But "if things are as bad on the tariff front in 3-6 months as they are now, then sure, we'll have a recession," Yardeni said.

But he expects political backlash for the tariff move, as a potential economic slowdown would hurt the Republican Party's chances in next year's midterm elections. 

"I'm expecting a slowdown in the second half of the year, but not an outright recession," Yardeni said. "I would say 45% is my number for the risk of stagflation and/or including the possibility of a mild recession."

Yardeni, who coined the terms "Fed model" in the 1990s and "bond vigilante" in the 1980s, maintained his target of 6,000 for the S&P 500 (SP500).

As for the Magnificent 7, which includes Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), and Microsoft (MSFT), Yardeni said: "I think they're still magnificent."

data.title: M&A snapshot: Brookfield to acquire Colonial Pipeline owner in $9 billion deal
European stocks extend losses after touching lowest level since mid-January, yields slump
China, EU revive EV tariff talks following U.S. trade actions
data.description:
China's commerce ministry stated on Thursday that China and the European Union have agreed to resume negotiations on electric-vehicle tariffs as soon as possible, in the wake of Trump's announcement of additional tariffs. 

The remarks were made by ministry spokesperson He Yadong at a press conference. He noted that the resumption of talks is part of efforts to foster a stable environment for investment and industrial collaboration between Chinese and EU enterprises.

This decision, announced by China's Commerce Ministry, comes in response to President Trump's declaration of an extra 34% duty on Chinese imports and a 20% tariff on EU imports. A separate 25% tariff on foreign automotive imports has also been part of the Trump administration's trade agenda.

Beijing-EU trade relations have also been strained for a while, with the EU trading bloc placing a higher tariff on EVs made in China last year, charging BYD at 17.0%, Geely at 18.8% and SAIC at 35.3%.The three Chinese manufacturers took the tariffs to the Court of Justice of the European Union in January.

Earlier, in November, China and the EU explored alternative solutions, including minimum EV price requirements.

EU-linked ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), and (NASDAQ:DAX).

China-linked ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE)

data.title: StubHub said to delay IPO once again after tariff-fueled selloff
data.description:
• StubHub (STUB) is delaying its IPO plans once again, sources told The Wall Street Journal, as the tariff-fueled stock market selloff has deterred the ticket resale platform from going public.
  • The company has put its IPO roadshow on hold, which was scheduled for next week, after the S&P 500 on Thursday saw its worst trading session since the COVID-19 pandemic.
  • StubHub (STUB) and its advisers were said to be concerned that potential investors would not participate in the roadshow or that they'd be distracted.
  • One of the sources told WSJ that there was also concern that moving ahead with the IPO at such a volatile time in the stock market might make StubHub (STUB) look desperate.
  • The company had considered an IPO last year, but it was delayed due to market conditions.
